author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XXIV. 16-18.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then let them which be in Judæa flee into the mountains.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And let him that is on the housetop not come down to take anything out of his house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Neither let him which is in his field return back to take his clothes.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having spoken of the ills that were to overtake the city, and of the trials of the apostles, and that they should remain unsubdued, and should overrun the whole world, He mentions again the Jews’ calamities, showing that when the one should be glorious, having taught the whole world, the others should be in calamity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see how He relates the war, by the things that seem to be small setting forth how intolerable it was to be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Then,” saith He, “let them which be in Judæa flee into the mountains.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When these things should be, “when the abomination of desolation should stand in the holy place.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence he seems to me to be speaking of the armies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Flee therefore then, saith He, for thenceforth there is no hope of safety for you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since it had fallen out, that they often had recovered themselves in grievous wars, as under Sennacherib, under Antiochus again (for when at that time also, armies had come in upon them, and the temple had been seized beforehand, the Maccabees rallying gave their affairs an opposite turn); in order then that they might not now also suspect this, that there would be any such change, He forbids them all thought of the kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it were well, saith He, to escape henceforth with one’s naked body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore them also that are on the housetop, He suffers not to enter into the house to take their clothes, indicating the evils to be inevitable, and the calamity without end, and that it must needs be that he that was involved therein should surely perish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He adds also, him that is in the field, saying, neither let this man turn back to take his clothes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if they that are in doors flee, much more they that are out of doors ought not to take refuge within.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Woe unto them that are with child, and to them that give suck,”to the one because of their greater inertness, and because they cannot flee easily, being weighed down by the burden of their pregnancy; to the other, because they are held by the tie of feeling for their children, and cannot save their sucklings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For money it is a light thing to despise, and an easy thing to provide, and clothes; but the bonds of nature how could any one escape? how could the pregnant woman become active? how could she that gives suck be able to overlook that which she had borne?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, to show again the greatness of the calamity, He saith, “Pray ye that your flight be not in the winter, neither on the Sabbath day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For then shall be great tribulation, such as was not since the beginning of the world until now, neither shall be.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou that His discourse is addressed to the Jews, and that He is speaking of the ills that should overtake them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the apostles surely were not to keep the Sabbath day, neither to be there, when Vespasian did those things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed the most part of them were already departed this life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if any was left, he was dwelling then in other parts of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wherefore neither “in the winter, nor on the Sabbath day?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not in the winter, because of the difficulty arising from the season; not on the Sabbath day, because of the absolute authority exercised by the law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since they had need of flight, and of the swiftest flight, but neither would the Jews dare to flee on the Sabbath day, because of the law, neither in winter was such a thing easy; therefore, “Pray ye,” saith He; “for then shall be tribulation, such as never was, neither shall be.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And let not any man suppose this to have been spoken hyperbolically; but let him study the writings of Josephus, and learn the truth of the sayings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither can any one say, that the man being a believer, in order to establish Christ’s words, hath exaggerated the tragical history.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed He was both a Jew, and a determined Jew, and very zealous, and among them that lived after Christ’s coming.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith this man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That those terrors surpassed all tragedy, and that no such had ever overtaken the nation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so great was the famine, that the very mothers fought about the devouring of their children, and that there were wars about this; and he saith that many when they were dead had their bellies ripped up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I should therefore be glad to inquire of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence came there thus upon them wrath from God intolerable, and more sore than all that had befallen aforetime, not in Judæa only, but in any part of the world?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is it not quite clear, that it was for the deed of the cross, and for this rejection?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: All would say it, and with all and before all the truth of the facts itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark, I pray thee, the exceeding greatness of the ills, when not only compared with the time before, they appear more grievous, but also with all the time to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not in all the world, neither in all time that is past, and that is to come, shall any one be able to say such ills have been.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And very naturally; for neither had any man perpetrated, not of those that ever have been, nor of those to come hereafter, a deed so wicked and horrible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He saith, “there shall be tribulation such as never was, nor shall be.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And except those days should be shortened, there should no flesh be saved; but for the elect’s sake those days shall be shortened.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By these things He shows them to be deserving of a more grievous punishment than had been mentioned, speaking now of the days of the war and of that siege.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what He saith is like this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If, saith He, the war of the Romans against the city had prevailed further, all the Jews had perished (for by “no flesh” here, He meaneth no Jewish flesh), both those abroad, and those at home.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not only against those in Judæa did they war, but also those that were dispersed everywhere they outlawed and banished, because of their hatred against the former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But whom doth He here mean by the elect?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The believers that were shut up in the midst of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that Jews may not say that because of the gospel, and the worship of Christ, these ills took place, He showeth, that so far from the believers being the cause, if it had not been for them, all had perished utterly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if God had permitted the war to be protracted, not so much as a remnant of the Jews had remained, but lest those of them who had become believers should perish together with the unbelieving Jews, He quickly put down the fighting, and gave an end to the war.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He saith, “But for the elect’s sake they shall be shortened.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But these things He said to leave an encouragement to those of them who were shut up in the midst of them, and to allow them to take breath, that they might not be in fear, as though they were to perish with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if here so great is His care for them, that for their sakes others also are saved, and that for the sake of Christians remnants were left of the Jews, how great will be their honor in the time for their crowns?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By this He also encouraged them not to be distressed at their own dangers, since these others are suffering such things, and for no profit, but for evil upon their own head.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He not only encouraged them, but also led them off secretly and unsuspectedly from the customs of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if there is not to be a change afterwards, and the temple is not to stand, it is quite evident that the law also shall be made to cease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, He spake not this openly, but by their entire destruction He darkly intimated it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He spake it not openly, lest He should startle them before the time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore neither at the beginning did He of Himself fall into discourse touching these things; but having first lamented over the city, He constrained them to show Him the stones, and question Him, in order that as it were in answering them their question, He might declare to them beforehand all the things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark thou, I pray thee, the dispensation of the Spirit, that John wrote none of these things, lest he should seem to write from the very history of the things done (for indeed he lived a long time after the taking of the city), but they, who died before the taking, and had seen none of these things, they write it, in order that every way the power of the prediction should clearly shine forth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then, if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is Christ, or there; believe it not: for there shall arise false Christs, and false prophets, and shall show signs and wonders, so as to deceive, if possible, the very elect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Behold, I have told you before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore if they shall say unto you, Behold, He is in the desert, go not forth: behold, He is in the secret chambers, believe it not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as the lightning cometh out of the east, and shineth even unto the west, so shall also the coming of the Son of Man be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For wheresoever the carcase is, there shall the eagles be gathered together.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having finished what concerned Jerusalem, He passes on to His own coming, and tells the signs of it, not for their use only, but for us also, and for all that shall come after us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Here, as I have often said, the word, “then,” relates not to the connection in order of time with the things before mentioned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At least, when He was minded to express the connection of time, He added, “Immediately after the tribulation of those days,”but here not so, but, “then,” not meaning what should follow straightway after these things, but what should be in the time, when these things were to be done, of which He was about to speak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So also when it is said, “In those days cometh John the Baptist,”he is not speaking of the time that should straightway follow, but that many years after, and that in which these things were done, of which He was about to speak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, in fact, having spoken of the birth of Jesus, and of the coming of the magi, and of the death of Herod, He at once saith, “In those days cometh John the Baptist;” although thirty years had intervened.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this is customary in the Scripture, I mean, to use this manner of narration.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So then here also, having passed over all the intermediate time from the taking of Jerusalem unto the preludes of the consummation, He speaketh of the time just before the consummation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then,” He saith therefore, “if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is Christ, or there, believe it not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Awhile He secures them by the place, mentioning the distinguishing marks of His second coming, and the indications of the deceivers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not, as when at His former coming He appeared in Bethlehem, and in a small corner of the world, and no one knew Him at the beginning, so doth He say it shall be then too; but openly and with all circumstance, and so as not to need one to tell these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is no small sign that He will not come secretly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark how here He saith nothing of war (for He is interpreting the doctrine concerning His advent), but of them that attempt to deceive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For some in the days of the apostles deceived the multitude, “for they shall come,” saith He, “and shall deceive many;”and others shall do so before His second coming, who shall also be more grievous than the former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For they shall show,” He saith, “signs and wonders, so as to deceive if possible the very elect:”here He is speaking of Antichrist, and indicates that some also shall minister to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of him Paul too speaks on this wise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having called him “man of sin,” and “son of perdition,” He added, “Whose coming is after the working of Satan, with all power and signs and lying wonders; and with all deceivableness of unrighteousness in them that perish.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see how He secures them; “Go not forth into the deserts, enter not into the secret chambers.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He did not say, “Go, and do not believe;” but, “Go not forth, neither depart thither.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For great then will be the deceiving, because that even deceiving miracles are wrought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having told them how Antichrist cometh, as, for instance, that it will be in a place; He saith how Himself also cometh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then doth He Himself come?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “As the lightning cometh out of the east, and shineth even unto the west, so shall also the coming of the Son of Man be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For wheresoever the carcase is, there also will the eagles be gathered together.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then shineth the lightning?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It needs not one to talk of it, it needs not a herald, but even to them that sit in houses, and to them in chambers it shows itself in an instant of time throughout the whole world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So shall that coming be, showing itself at once everywhere by reason of the shining forth of His glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He mentions also another sign, “where the carcase is, there also shall the eagles be;” meaning the multitude of the angels, of the martyrs, of all the saints.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then He tells of fearful prodigies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What are these prodigies?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Immediately after the tribulation of those days,” saith He, “the sun shall be darkened.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of the tribulation of what days doth He speak?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of those of Antichrist and of the false prophets?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there shall be great tribulation, there being so many deceivers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But it is not protracted to a length of time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if the Jewish war was shortened for the elect’s sake, much more shall this temptation be limited for these same’s sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, He said not, “after the tribulation,” but Immediately “after the tribulation of those days shall the sun be darkened,” for almost at the same time all things come to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the false prophets and false Christs shall come and cause confusion, and immediately He Himself will be here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because no small turmoil is then to prevail over the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But how doth He come?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The very creation being then transfigured, for “the sun shall be darkened,” not destroyed, but overcome by the light of His presence; and the stars shall fall, for what shall be the need of them thenceforth, there being no night? and “the powers of Heaven shall be shaken,” and in all likelihood, seeing so great a change come to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if when the stars were made, they trembled and marvelled (“for when the stars were made, all angels,” it is said, “praised Me with a loud voice”);much more seeing all things in course of change, and their fellow servants giving account, and the whole world standing by that awful judgment-seat, and those who have lived from Adam unto His coming, having an account demanded of them of all that they did, how shall they but tremble, and be shaken?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then shall appear the sign of the Son of Man in Heaven;”that is, the cross being brighter than the sun, since this last will be darkened, and hide himself, and that will appear when it would not appear, unless it were far brighter than the beams of the sun.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wherefore doth the sign appear?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In order that the shamelessness of the Jews may be more abundantly silenced.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For having the cross as the greatest plea, Christ thus cometh to that judgment-seat, showing not His wounds only, but also the death of reproach.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then shall the tribes mourn,” for there shall be no need of an accusation, when they see the cross; and they shall mourn, that by His death they are nothing benefited; because they crucified Him whom they ought to have adored.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how fearfully He has pictured His coming? how He has stirred up the spirits of His disciples?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this reason, let me add, He puts the mournful things first, and then the good things, that in this way also He may comfort and refresh them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And of His passion He suggests to them the remembrance, and of His resurrection, and with a display of glory, He mentions His cross, so that they may not be ashamed nor grieve, whereas indeed He cometh then setting it forth for His sign.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And another saith, “They shall look on Him whom they pierced.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore it is that they shall mourn, when they see that this is He.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And forasmuch as He had made mention of the cross, He added, “They shall see the Son of Man coming,” no longer on the cross, but “in the clouds of Heaven, with power and great glory.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For think not, He meaneth, because thou hearest of the cross, that it is again anything mournful, for He shall come with power and great glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He bringeth it, that their sin may be self-condemned, as if any one who had been struck by a stone, were to show the stone itself, or his garments stained with blood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He cometh in a cloud as He was taken up, and the tribes seeing these things mourn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not however that the terrors shall with them proceed no further than mournings; but the mourning shall be, that they may bring forth their sentence from within, and condemn themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And then again, “He will send His angels with a great trumpet,and they shall gather the elect from the four winds, from one end of Heaven to the other.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when thou hast heard of this, consider the punishment of them that remain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither shall they suffer that former penalty only, but this too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as above He said, that they should say, “Blessed is He that cometh in the name of the Lord,”so here, that they shall mourn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since He had spoken unto them of grievous wars, that they might learn, that together with the fearful things here, the torments there also await them, He brings them in mourning and separated from the elect, and consigned to hell; by this again rousing the disciples, and indicating from how many evils they should be delivered, and how many good things they shall enjoy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why now doth He call them by angels, if He comes thus openly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To honor them in this way also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Paul saith, that they “shall be caught up in clouds.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He said this also, when He was speaking concerning a resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Forthe Lord Himself,” it is said, “shall descend from Heaven with a shout, with the voice of an archangel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that when risen again, the angels shall gather them together, when gathered together the clouds shall catch them up; and all these things are done in a moment, in an instant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is not that He abiding above calleth them, but He Himself cometh with the sound of a trumpet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what mean the trumpets and the sound?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They are for arousing, for gladness, to set forth the amazing nature of the things then doing, for grief to them that are left.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Woe is me for that fearful day!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though we ought to rejoice when we hear these things, we feel pain, and are dejected, and our countenance is sad.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or is it I only that feel thus, and do ye rejoice at hearing of these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For upon me at least there comes a kind of shudder when these things are said, and I lament bitterly, and groan from the very depth of my heart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I have no part in these things, but in those that are spoken afterwards, that are said unto the virgins, unto him that buried the talents he had received, unto the wicked servant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause I weep, to think from what glory we are to be cast out, from what hope of blessings, and this perpetually, and forever, to spare ourselves a little labor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if indeed this were a great toil, and a grievous law, we ought even so to do all things; nevertheless many of the remiss would seem to have at least some pretext, a poor pretext indeed, yet would they seem to have some, that the toil was great, and the time endless, and the burden intolerable; but now we can put forward no such objection; which circumstance most of all will gnawus no less than hell at that time, when for want of a slight endeavor, and a little toil, we shall have lost Heaven, and the unspeakable blessings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For both the time is short, and the labor small, and yet we faint and are supine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou strivest on earth, and the crown is in Heaven; thou art punished of men, and art honored of God; the race is for two days, and the reward for endless ages; the struggle is a corruptible body, and the rewards in an incorruptible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And apart from these things, we should consider another point also, that even if we do not choose to suffer any of the things that are painful for Christ’s sake, we must in other ways most assuredly endure them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither, though thou shouldest not have died for Christ, wilt thou be immortal; neither though thou shouldest not have cast away thy riches for Christ, wilt thou go away hence with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These things He requires of thee, which although He should not require them, thou wilt have to give up, because thou art mortal; He willeth thee to do these by thy choice, which thou must do by necessity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So much only He requires to be added, that it be done for His sake; since that these things befall men and pass away, cometh to pass of natural necessity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how easy the conflict?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What it is altogether necessary for thee to suffer, that choose to suffer for my sake; let this only be added, and I have sufficient obedience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The gold which thou intendest to lend to another, this lend to me, both at more profit, and in greater security.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thy body, wherewith thou art going to warfare for another, make it to war for me, for indeed I surpass thy toils with recompenses in the most abundant excess.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet thou in all other matters preferrest him that giveth thee more as well in loans, as in marketing and in warfare; but Christ alone, when giving more, and infinitely more than all, thou dost not receive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is this so great hostility?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is this so great enmity?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Where will there be any excuse or defense left for thee, when the reasons for which thou preferrest man to man avail not to induce thee to prefer God to man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou commit thy treasure to the earth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Give it into my hand,” He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Doth not the earth’s Lord seem to thee more worthy of trust than the earth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This indeed restoreth that which thou laidest in it, though oftentimes not even this, but He gives thee also recompense for His keeping of it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed He doth exceedingly love us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore if thou shouldest wish to lend, He stands ready; or to sow, He receives it; or if thou shouldest wish to build, He draws thee unto Himself, saying, Build in my regions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why runnest thou unto poor, unto beggarly men, who also for little gains occasion thee great trouble?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless, not even on hearing these things, do we make up our minds to it, but where are fightings and wars, and wild struggles,and trials and suits of law, and false accusations, thither do we hasten.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Doth He not justly turn away from us, and punish us, when He is giving up Himself unto us for all things, and we are resisting Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is surely plain to all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whether thou art desirous to adorn thyself, “Let it, He saith, be with my ornaments;” or to arm thyself, “with my arms,” or to clothe thyself, “with my raiment;” or to feed thyself, “at my table;” or to journey, “on my way;” or to inherit, “my inheritance;” or to enter into a country, “the city of which I am builder and maker;” or to build a house, “amongst my tabernacles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For I, so far from asking thee for a recompense of the things that I give thee, to even make myself owe thee a recompense for this very thing, if thou be willing to use all I have.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What can be equal to this munificence, “I am Father, I am brother, I am bridegroom, I am dwelling place, I am food, I am raiment, I am root, I am foundation, all whatsoever thou willest, I am.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Be thou in need of nothing, I will be even a servant, for I came to minister, not to be ministered unto; I am friend, and member, and head, and brother, and sister, and mother; I am all; only cling thou closelyto me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I was poor for thee, and a wanderer for thee, on the cross for thee, in the tomb for thee, above I intercede for thee to the Father; on earth I am come for thy sake am ambassador from my Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou art all things to me, brother, and joint heir, and friend, and member.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What wouldest thou more?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou turn away from Him, who loveth thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou labor for the world?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou draw water into a broken cistern?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is this to labor for the present life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou comb wool into the fire?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou “beat the air?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou “run in vain?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hath not every art an end?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is surely plain to every one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do thou also show the end of thy worldly eagerness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thou canst not; for, “vanity of vanities, all is vanity.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us go to the tombs; show me thy father; show me thy wife.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Where is he that was clad in raiment of gold? he that rode in the chariot? he that had armies, that had the girdle,that had the heralds? he that was slaying these, and casting those into prison? he that put to death whom he would, and set free whom he was minded?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I see nothing but bones, and a worm, and a spider’s web; all those things are earth, all those a fable, all a dream, and a shadow, and a bare relation, and a picture, or rather not so much as a picture.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the picture we see at least in a likeness, but here not so much as a likeness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And would that the evils stop with this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For now the honor, and the luxury, and the distinction, end with a shadow, with words; but the consequences of them, are no longer limited to a shadow and to words, but continue, and will pass over with us elsewhere, and will be manifest to all, the rapine, the covetousness, the fornications, the adulteries, the dreadful things beyond number; these not in similitude, neither in ashes, but written above, both words and deeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With what eyes then shall we behold Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if any one could not bear to see his father, when conscious to himself that he had sinned against him, upon Him who infinitely exceeds a father in forbearance how shall we then look? how shall we bear it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed we shall stand at Christ’s judgment-seat, and there will be a strict inquiry into all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if any man disbelieve the judgments to come, let him look at the things here, at those in the prisons, those in the mines, those on the dunghills, the possessed, the frantic, them that are struggling with incurable diseases, those that are fighting against continual poverty, them that live in famine, them that are pierced with irremediable woes, those in captivity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these persons would not suffer these things here, unless vengeance and punishments were to await all the others also that have committed such sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if the rest have undergone nothing here, you ought to regard this very fact as a sign that there is surely something to follow after our departure here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the self-same God of all would not take vengeance on some, and leave others unpunished, who have committed the same or more grievous offenses, unless He designed to bring some punishments upon them there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By these arguments then and these examples let us also humble ourselves; and let them who are obstinate unbelievers of the judgment believe it henceforth, and become better men; that having lived here in a manner worthy of the kingdom, we may attain unto the good things to come, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory forever and ever.
